status: OPEN. Theorems below are about residue CLASSES and bounded ranges, not the conjecture itself.

T1_prime_reduction_lemma.statement: If m | n and 4/m = 1/a + 1/b + 1/c (a,b,c ∈ ℤ⁺), then 4/n = 1/(a·n/m) + 1/(b·n/m) + 1/(c·n/m).
T1_prime_reduction_lemma.corollary: It suffices to solve 4/p for the smallest prime factor p of n. p ∈ {2,3} or p ≡ 3 (mod 4) have O(1) identities, so only primes p ≡ 1 (mod 4) ever require search.

T2_verified_witnesses.note: Every n ≤ 10^6 has a witness re-checked by the independent verifier; equivalently every prime ≤ 10^6 is certified, which by T1 certifies every n ≤ 10^6. (Literature: verified past 10^17.)

T3_certified_identities.degree_profile: x: degree 1, y: degree 2, z: degree 4 (uniform)

polynomial_identity.claim: 4*x*y*z - n*(y*z + x*z + x*y) == 0  for all k

integrality.argument: P takes integer values at deg+1 consecutive integers, so by the integer-valued-polynomial theorem P(k) ∈ ℤ for all k ∈ ℤ; the forward differences Δ^j P(0) (integers) are the witness.

positivity.argument: every coefficient ≥ 0 and the constant term > 0, hence P(k) > 0 for all k ≥ 0.

T4_coverage_reduction.target_description: residues r mod 840 with gcd(r,840)=1 and r ≡ 1 (mod 4)
T4_coverage_reduction.statement: Mining reduces the residue classes still needing a per-value search from 96 to 14 (mod 840).

A_known_mordell_frontier.status: genuinely open; no Erdős–Straus identity is known here.

diagnosis.interpolation_degree: NOT the cause. The mined families are degree (1,2,4); re-running with up to 24 sample points (enough to fit degree ≤ 23) still finds nothing for these 8, so the fit is not degree-limited.
diagnosis.ansatz: THE cause. The miner's ansatz fixes the first term as x = 210k + c (x ≈ n/4) and takes the canonical two-term split of the remainder. For these 8 classes no offset c yields a polynomial (y(k), z(k)); alternative single-slopes (280, 420, 168, 120) also fail.
diagnosis.missing_known_identities: Mordell's and Salez's identity sets DO cover these residues, but via forms outside this ansatz (first term not ≈ n/4, or different splits).
diagnosis.richer_families_required: Capturing them needs a multi-parameter ansatz (e.g. x = a·k + c with a search over a, or a 2-parameter (k, j) family). That is the natural next extension; it is NOT evidence the classes are hard — unlike the 6 frontier classes, identities for these 8 are known to exist.
